name: Chocolate Almond Flourless Torte
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces good imported bittersweet chocolate, broken into pieces
1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter or margarine, at room temperature
1/2 cup granulated sugar plus more for sprinkling
1/3 cup finely ground almonds (done in a food processor)
2 tablespoons kosher for Passover cognac or dark rum
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit and grease well a 9-inch springform pan. Line the bottom with parchment paper.
2. Melt chocolate in a double boiler over hot, not boiling water. Turn off heat and let cool slowly.
3. In a large bowl, beat butter with 1/4 cup sugar until fluffy and almost white. Add egg yolks and beat for 1 minute. Add almonds and cognac; beat 2 more minutes.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until light and foamy. Gradually add remaining 1/4 cup sugar. Beat until stiff and shiny.
5. Add melted chocolate to egg yolk mixture and mix with a rubber spatula until well combined. Fold in 1/4 of this chocolate mixture into the egg whites; then gradually fold egg whites back into the rest of the chocolate mixture, taking care not to deflate batter.
6. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until a tester comes out with a thick, moist, not wet, crumbly coating. Allow cake to cool in the pan for 30 minutes.
7. Loosen edges with a knife and carefully turn cake onto a plate. Remove parchment paper. Sprinkle with sugar. Serve at room temperature or chilled with whipped cream and raspberries on the side.
Yield: 10-12 servings
